cases.manual_explanation: The START condition is inverted, so the OVF-clear branch fires when START=0 and is suppressed when START=1, leaving OVF asserted one cycle after a valid START pulse.
The equality polarity of the sign-extension check is flipped from != to ==, so OVF is spuriously set when the MSBs are equal (safe shift) instead of when they differ (true overflow).
The enable-data gate predicate is inverted, so the RDY and OVF update block executes when ED=0 instead of ED=1, leaving RDY stuck at 1 when a valid input arrives with ED asserted.
Reset polarity is inverted so the FSM executes its normal operational logic while rst=1, leaving all outputs in an undefined non-zero state immediately after the reset cycle instead of zeroing them.

The counter-done condition is inverted so the FSM advances only when cnt_done is low and stalls when it is high, preventing the transition from sync_state to gdel_state and so Gate never rises within MaxGateRiseDelay.
cases.adjudication_note: SubAgent A agreed: missing_state_transition is more precise than timing_progression_error here — the FSM is structurally stuck, not just slow.

cases.original_snippet: wire baud_tick = (baud_cnt == BaudDiv - 1);    // End of bit period
cases.mutated_snippet: wire baud_tick = (baud_cnt != BaudDiv - 1);    // End of bit period
cases.manual_explanation: BaudDiv-1 terminal count equality is flipped to inequality, making baud_tick asserted for all cycles except the last, so the bit sampler fires continuously at the wrong phase and corrupts the received byte.

The baud-tick handshake predicate is inverted so the shift register samples on every non-tick cycle instead of on the single terminal tick, shifting bits at the wrong phase and producing a corrupted received byte.
Truncating the shift-register slice from i[9:0] to i[8:0] drops the MSB of the permutation round counter, so the completion flag never propagates to the output-ready signal within the LIVENESS_BOUND.

metric_definitions.correct_cycle: CNS identifies the first_bad_cycle within ±1 cycle of ground truth
metric_definitions.correct_signal: CNS names at least one signal from key_signals as the direct cause
metric_definitions.correct_mechanism: CNS assigns a root_cause_class matching ground truth
metric_definitions.unsupported_claims: count of signals or events cited in CNS output not present in trace
metric_definitions.overall_faithful: correct_cycle AND correct_signal AND correct_mechanism AND unsupported_claims == 0
